overview: Avatar: The Last Airbender is a faction-based set focused on elemental bending and heroic alliances. The format features 10 distinct archetypes based on the Four Nations and the struggle against the Fire Nation. It is a synergy-heavy environment that rewards careful deckbuilding around specific mechanics like Bending, Lessons, and Allies.

mechanics.name: Bending
mechanics.description: Activated abilities on creatures that trigger additional effects (firebending, earthbending, etc.).
mechanics.draft_tip: Bending usually requires a specific color or mana investment. Look for creatures with bending abilities that stabilize the board or provide reach.

mechanics.name: Lessons
mechanics.description: Noncreature spells that can be fetched from the sideboard or played for additional martial prowess.
mechanics.draft_tip: Drafting 'Lesson' cards early allows your 'Learn' effects to be more versatile. They are essential for the UR and GU archetypes.

mechanics.name: Allies
mechanics.description: A creature type synergy that rewards playing multiple 'Ally' cards to go wide.
mechanics.draft_tip: Allies are strongest in GW and RW. The more you have, the more explosive your turns become.

mechanics.name: Draw Two
mechanics.description: Triggered abilities that activate when you draw your second card each turn.
mechanics.draft_tip: Clues and card-draw spells are the engine here. Prioritize repeatable draw effects to keep the bonuses flowing.

archetypes.name: Flyers
archetypes.description: Take to the skies with Aang and his friends! Gather a team of flyers and soar like an Airbender.
archetypes.strategy: Draft evasive creatures and tempo spells. Use the air to bypass ground stalls typical of the Earth Kingdom.

archetypes.name: Draw Two
archetypes.description: Hunt for knowledge, using Clues and other draw effects to trigger your second-draw reward cards.
archetypes.strategy: The long game is yours! Use Clues to trigger second-draw bonuses and out-value your opponent over time.

archetypes.name: Fire Nation Aggro
archetypes.description: Be aggressive with firebending and burn it on extra effects.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out aggressively. Use bending triggers to deal direct damage and clear blockers to maintain pressure.

archetypes.name: Earth Rumble Ramp
archetypes.description: Use mana ramp and earthbending to create 4+ power creatures, then rack up the bonuses to claim the title belt.
archetypes.strategy: Focus on high-toughness creatures and ramp spells. Once you have a 4+ power creature, your 'Earth Rumble' bonuses activate.

archetypes.name: Allies
archetypes.description: Play Allies and generate tokens to go wide and hit your enemies hard from all sides.
archetypes.strategy: A world united is unstoppable. Focus on the Ally creature type to trigger mutual buffs and token generation.

archetypes.name: Sacrifice
archetypes.description: Play small creatures and clues, then gain the upper hand when you sacrifice them.
archetypes.strategy: Victory comes at a cost. Use Clues and small Allies as fuel for your sacrifice outlets to grind out games.

archetypes.name: Combat Lessons
archetypes.description: Play creatures that care about casting noncreature spells, then teach your foes a lesson with martial prowess.
archetypes.strategy: Draft a high count of noncreature spells. Use them to trigger 'Prowess'-like effects on your benders.

archetypes.name: +1/+1 Counters
archetypes.description: Accrue power by any means! Stack up counters to gain bonuses and control the Earth Kingdom from within.
archetypes.strategy: Stack up +1/+1 counters to build massive threats that dominate combat and trigger Earth Kingdom rewards.

archetypes.name: Go Wide Aggro
archetypes.description: Lead the rebellion with an army of Allies and tokens that you can enhance for devastating attacks.
archetypes.strategy: Combine Red's aggression with White's token generation. Use team-wide buffs to end games quickly.

archetypes.name: Ramp + Lessons
archetypes.description: Ramp and draw cards to play big creatures while getting Lessons in your graveyard.
archetypes.strategy: Turn your knowledge into power. Ramp into huge creatures while using Lessons to maintain utility and board control.

general_tips: Everything changes when the Fire Nation attacks — be prepared for aggressive starts in the BR and RW archetypes.
Bending abilities are mana sinks. Don't over-commit your mana until you know you don't need a counterspell or protection.
Lessons provide incredible flexibility. Having even 2-3 in your sideboard makes your 'Learn' effects twice as good.
Spirit Portals are essential if you want to splash for Aang or other multi-color bombs.
Clues are the backbone of the Blue-Black and White-Black decks. Don't sacrifice them too early.
